methods.description: Setting up and running climate simulations using the Community Earth System Model version 2 (CESM2) with specific configurations (preindustrial control, full LGM, LGM ice sheets only) and resolution, prescribing LGM ice sheets using the ICE-6G reconstruction, and running for a specific duration after spin-up.

steps.description: Configure CESM2 for a preindustrial control simulation.
Configure CESM2 for a full LGM simulation, including lower greenhouse gas concentrations and modified insolation.
Configure CESM2 for a simulation with LGM ice sheets but otherwise the same as preindustrial.
steps.searchable_summary: configure CESM2 LGM ice sheets only simulation
outputs: CESM2 configuration for LGM ice sheets only

steps.description: Set the model horizontal resolution to 0.9° × 1.25° for the atmosphere/land and 1° for the ocean/sea ice in all simulations.
Prescribe the LGM ice sheet using the ICE-6G reconstruction at 21 ka in the two relevant cases (full LGM and LGM ice sheets only simulations).
steps.searchable_summary: prescribe LGM ice sheet boundary condition using ICE-6G
outputs: CESM2 configuration with prescribed LGM ice sheet

steps.description: Run simulations for a period of spin-up (details in Text S1).
Run simulations for 100 years after the spin-up period for analysis.
steps.searchable_summary: run CESM2 production simulation for 100 years
outputs: CESM2 simulation output (climate variables for 100 years)

methods.description: Applying a two-sided t-test to determine the statistical significance of differences between simulation results and filtering results to show only significant differences.

steps.description: Perform a two-sided t-test on differences between simulations.
steps.searchable_summary: perform two-sided t-test on simulation differences
inputs: Simulation results (e.g., variable differences between simulations)

steps.description: Filter results to display only significant differences (p < 0.01).

methods.description: Defining and calculating extreme precipitation as the all-interval 99th percentile locally using 6-hourly precipitation data from simulations.

steps.searchable_summary: load 6-hourly precipitation data from simulation output

steps.description: Calculate the value of precipitation at the all-interval 99th percentile locally for each simulation.
Compare the calculated 99th percentile values between simulations to show the change in that value.
steps.searchable_summary: compare extreme precipitation values between simulations
inputs: Local 99th percentile precipitation values from different simulations

methods.description: Identifying Atmospheric Rivers using the "Lora_v2" algorithm based on 6-hourly integrated vapor transport (IVT) regions that exceed a threshold value, which depends on the 30-day running mean of the zonally averaged integrated water vapor.

steps.description: Obtain 6-hourly integrated vapor transport (IVT) data from simulations.
Calculate the 30-day running mean of the zonally averaged integrated water vapor.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate 30-day running mean of zonally averaged integrated water vapor
outputs: 30-day running mean zonally averaged integrated water vapor

steps.description: Determine a threshold value for IVT based on the 30-day running mean of the zonally averaged integrated water vapor, accounting for background climate state.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate IVT threshold based on background climate state
inputs: 30-day running mean zonally averaged integrated water vapor

steps.description: Identify AR regions using the algorithm ("Lora_v2") where 6-hourly IVT exceeds the calculated threshold.
steps.searchable_summary: apply Lora_v2 algorithm to identify AR regions based on IVT threshold

methods.description: Defining Atmospheric River (AR) IVT and AR precipitation simply as IVT and precipitation that are concurrent with identified AR regions.

steps.description: Load identified AR regions from the AR identification method.
inputs: Atmospheric River (AR) mask/regions from AR Identification method

steps.description: Extract IVT values that are concurrent with the identified AR regions.
steps.searchable_summary: extract IVT values within AR regions

steps.description: Extract precipitation values that are concurrent with the identified AR regions.
steps.searchable_summary: extract precipitation values within AR regions
